description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone with a predominantly beige base and a dark brown, textured upper surface. The top of the stone features a series of parallel, wavy ridges that give it a distinctive, almost fossil-like appearance. The sides are irregular and slightly rounded, with some subtle discoloration and natural imperfections visible throughout. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its surface is matte and rough, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The coloration and texture indicate it could be a piece of sedimentary rock, possibly used as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description: A dense, rectangular stone with a beige body and dark brown, ridged top surface, featuring rough, weathered textures.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's sloped, ridged top and one of its broader, slightly uneven sides.
description_view_1: The ridged pattern on the top is most prominent here, with the stone's length and surface texture clearly visible.
description_view_2: This angle shows the stone's tapering end and the transition from the dark top to the lighter, rougher side.
description_view_3: The stone's broad, flat side is visible, with minimal ridging and a mostly uniform beige coloration.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Parallel, wavy ridges on the top surface with irregular, natural mottling on the sides
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible surface imperfections and discoloration
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements
